key_positions: Committed to achieving full climate neutrality across all member states by 2050 under the European Green Deal.
Targeting a minimum 55 percent net reduction in greenhouse gas emissions by 2030 compared to 1990 baseline levels.
Leading the global transition to renewable energy through large-scale deployment of offshore wind, green hydrogen, and solar photovoltaic.
Implementing the Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism to prevent carbon leakage and maintain competitive fairness in trade.
Advocating for robust, transparent carbon markets and the global phasing out of fossil fuel subsidies as a matter of urgency.
red_lines: Rejects any global climate framework that does not include legally binding and independently verifiable emissions reduction commitments.
Will not accept arrangements that permit major emitting economies to indefinitely defer meaningful decarbonisation action.
Opposes trade agreements with parties that systematically undermine agreed international environmental and climate standards.
